mission.title: thanos: Upload container images for arm64 architecture
mission.description: Upload container images for arm64 architecture. This issue affects 20+ users.

steps.title: Identify thanos feature symptoms
steps.description:
Check for the issue in your thanos deployment:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n cert-manager -l app=thanos
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=thanos -n cert-manager --tail=100 | grep -i error
```
Look for errors related to: Upload container images for arm64 architecture

steps.title: Check current resource configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant thanos resources:
```bash
kubectl get resource -A
kubectl describe resource <name> -n <namespace>
```
Now that AWS announced their way more powerful M6g instances it will start to get interesting to move from x86 to the ARM architecture. You already provide binaries on the releases page, but the docke

steps.title: Apply the fix for Upload container images for arm64 architecture
steps.description:
* [x] I added CHANGELOG entry for this change.

## Changes

Fix #1851

See the fix PR for details: linked PR

steps.title: Confirm Upload container images for arm64 architecture is resolved
steps.description:
Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=thanos -n cert-manager --tail=50 --since=5m
kubectl get events -n cert-manager --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm that the issue symptoms are gone.

resolution.summary:

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with thanos installed or the issue environment reproducible.
